description: A role record in The Ptah Protocol. The template that sits between the World and the Character. A shared identity that multiple Character instances can embody. Hamlet is a Role — each performance of Hamlet is a Character instance.

main.description: A stage role vs a performance of that role. The Opening of the Mouth was performed on a type of figure — the Role is the type. The Character instance is the specific statue that gets its mouth opened.

worldReference.description: The AT URI of the world record this role exists in.

creatorDID.description: Who defined this role in the protocol.

description.description: What this role is in the world's context — its narrative function, its archetypal weight.

sourceType.description: The intellectual property origin of this role.

sourceReference.description: If sourceType is publicDomain, the specific source material.

canonicalCharacterReference.description: The AT URI of the single Character record designated as the authoritative expression of this role. When present, this is the canonical instance.

canonicalReferencePolicy.description: Governs how the canonicalCharacterReference behaves over time.

instancePolicy.description: How instances of this role are governed.

authorshipRecord.description: Permanent link to the role's creator. Provenance travels.

canonicalStatus.description: The canonical standing of this role within its world.
